channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Mysterious Case of Luigi Mangione: A Social Media Detective Story
item.description: The murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson and the subsequent manhunt for alleged killer Luigi Mangione sparked a social media frenzy as people scoured his online presence for clues.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Mozilla is removing Firefox’s anti-tracking function
item.description: Mozilla has announced that Do Not Track, a feature designed to prevent user tracking online, will be removed with version 135 of the company’s Firefox browser. The decision comes as more and more websites choose to ignore Do Not Track, leaving users with a false sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Pentagon announces new AI office as it looks to deploy autonomous weapons
item.description: The Defense Department on Wednesday announced a new office focused on accelerating and adopting artificial intelligence (AI) technology for the military as it aims to deploy autonomous weapons in the near future.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Firefox Ends Support for Do Not Track: A Blow to Privacy or a New Beginning?
item.description: Firefox discontinues its Do Not Track feature, but is it really a loss for your privacy? Discover better alternatives and robust privacy features in this article.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI app helps you turn anything into LEGO models
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rose Wang says Bluesky is so much more than an X clone
item.description: A huge shift in social media is underway: People are leaving X while millions have flocked to Bluesky, a new upstart powered by a tiny 20-person team. Bluesky’s COO, Rose Wang, addresses this inflection point as dissatisfaction with major platforms continues to grow.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows investors' class action to proceed against microchip company Nvidia
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a class-action lawsuit that accuses Nvidia of misleading investors about its past dependence on selling computer chips for the mining of volatile cryptocurrency to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: GM Abandons Robotaxi Development, Shifts Focus to Autonomous Personal Vehicles
item.description: General Motors (GM) will no longer fund robotaxi development, instead focusing on fully autonomous personal vehicles.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: OpenSilver 3.1 brings XAML designer for VS Code
item.description: OpenSilver 3.1, the latest version of Userware’s open-source framework for cross-platform .NET development, has arrived with what the company calls the first-ever drag-and-drop XAML designer for Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft to launch zero water consumption cooling for future data centers
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new design for data centers to optimize artificial intelligence (AI) workloads, implementing a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gentrace makes it easier for businesses to test AI-powered software
item.description: As businesses continue to integrate generative AI into their products, many find it challenging to actually test whether the AI is behaving correctly and giving useful answers.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Fortnite players 'tricked' into unwanted purchases are starting to get refunds. Here's how to apply
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. consumers who were “tricked” into purchases they didn't want from Fortnite maker Epic Games are now starting to receive refund checks, the Federal Trade Commission said this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Not so ‘Wicked’ after all. Find authenticity in the era of AI
item.description: I recently had the opportunity to see Wicked, the cinematic adaptation of the acclaimed Broadway musical. Directed by Jon M. Chu, this film serves as the first installment of a two-part series that explores the origins of the beloved characters before the events of The Wizard of Oz.
item.dc:creator: R. Vann Graves, Fast Company

item.title: Kylie Kelce unseats Joe Rogan as top podcast with debut episode
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of NFL player-turned-media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Study: Scientists make more discoveries with help from AI
item.description: A new study by a PhD student at MIT indicates that AI tools can help scientists make more discoveries, according to Nature. In the study by Aidan Toner-Rodgers, an unspecified laboratory in the US with 1,018 researchers used an unspecified custom machine-learning tool.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Boosting Cyprus' defense is key so close to the war-wracked Mideast, defense minister says
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Bolstering Cyprus ' defense capacity is critical for the east Mediterranean island nation so close to the war-wrecked Middle East, the country's defense minister said Tuesday. The priority for Cyprus is to procure better defense systems, said Defense Minister Vasilis Palmas.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Working with PDF files on Linux
item.description: The PDF (portable document format) standard was created in the 1990s by Adobe cofounder John Warnock to make it possible for people to share reliably formatted documents across a wide range of platforms. It became an open ISO standard in 2015.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Best Places to Work in IT 2025
item.description: What makes a company a great place to work for IT professionals? Top salaries and benefits certainly help, but those are just table stakes. Tech workers are looking for challenging projects, growth opportunities, and continuous learning in a supportive workplace.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IT at Tokio Marine means continuous learning — and many hats
item.description: The insurance industry is known for being slow and steady, but at Tokio Marine North America Services (TMNAS), a dogged emphasis on growth, diversity of thought, and continuous learning has helped accelerate an IT culture predicated on innovation and career mobility.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China launches anti-monopoly probe into Nvidia amid rising US-China chip tensions
item.description: China has initiated an investigation into Nvidia over alleged violations of the country’s anti-monopoly laws, signaling a potential escalation in the ongoing tech and trade tensions between Beijing and Washington.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia's stock dips after China opens probe of the AI chip company for violating anti-monopoly laws
item.description: Shares of Nvidia fell Monday after China said it is investigating the high-flying U.S. microchip company over suspected violations of Chinese anti-monopoly laws.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: HPE execs reveal why they cancelled $700M AI server deal
item.description: There was one particular juicy tidbit during Hewlett Packard Enterprise’s (HPE’s) fourth quarter earnings call with financial analysts last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California to consider requiring mental health warnings on social media sites
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California, home to some of the largest technology companies in the world, would be the first U.S. state to require mental health warning labels on social media sites if lawmakers pass a bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot Vision assistant can now browse the web with you
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot Vision feature is now available for users to test in a limited preview. Built natively into Microsoft’s Edge browser, Copilot Vision analyzes and understands the contents of web pages you visit.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon seals Mountain View deal as South Bay office sector perks up
item.description: Amazon has struck a deal to rent a big office building in Mountain View in a hopeful sign of an office market rebound.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: IBM optical chip prototype aims to speed AI computing and slash data center energy demands
item.description: IBM researchers are developing optical chips that are expected to significantly speed connectivity in data center systems, enabling better performance and lower energy costs for customers that need to build and support AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX
item.description: Michelyne LeBlanc, the deputy CIO of the Defense Commissary Agency, said the move to zero trust is all about ensuring customer confidence in its services. The post Defense Commissary Agency striving to balance cyber, CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Supply chain compromise of Ultralytics AI library results in trojanized versions
item.description: Attackers have compromised Ultralytics YOLO packages published on PyPI, the official Python package index, by compromising the build environment of the popular library for creating custom machine learning models.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Don't get fooled by fake phone updates and notifications
item.description: Updates to apps are essential for maintaining the performance and security of your devices, including bug fixes, new features and security patches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's Nuclear and Space Programmes: A Growing Concern
item.description: Tensions between Iran and Western nations are escalating due to concerns over its rapidly expanding nuclear programme.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clarifai previews AI compute orchestration
item.description: AI platform provider Clarifai is publicly previewing compute orchestration for AI workloads that the company says works across any AI model on any compute, at any scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
